=== PLAN ===
# COR24 APL Interpreter -- Implementation Plan

## Phase Overview

| Phase | Description                    | Deliverable                       |
|-------|-------------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| 0     | Project setup and toolchain   | Build system, hello-world on emu  |
| 1     | Scalar REPL                   | Read, eval, print scalar exprs    |
| 2     | Vector support                | 1D arrays, element-wise ops       |
| 3     | Core APL primitives           | iota, rho, reduce, take, drop     |
| 4     | Matrix support                | 2D arrays, formatted output       |
| 5     | Polish and testing            | Error handling, edge cases, docs  |

---

## Phase 0: Project Setup and Toolchain Validation

### Step 0.1: Repository structure
- Create `src/` directory for C source files
- Create `asm/` directory for hand-written assembly helpers
- Create `test/` directory for test scripts
- Create `Makefile` or build script for tc24r + cor24-run pipeline
- Verify tc24r can compile a trivial C program to COR24 assembly
- Verify cor24-run can assemble and execute the result

### Step 0.2: UART I/O bootstrap
- Write `uart_putchar()` and `uart_getchar()` in C (or asm)
- Write `uart_puts()` for string output
- Write `uart_getline()` with backspace support
- Test: print "COR24 APL v0.1" on startup, echo typed lines

### Step 0.3: Number I/O
- Write `print_int()` -- integer to decimal string via UART
- Write `parse_int()` -- decimal string to integer
- Handle negative numbers (underscore prefix `_N`)
- Test: type a number, see it echoed back

---

## Phase 1: Scalar REPL

### Step 1.1: Tokenizer
- Implement token types: NUM, PLUS, MINUS, STAR, SLASH, LPAREN,
  RPAREN, ASSIGN, IDENT, RESERVED, EOL
- Scan input buffer into token array
- Reserved word lookup table (rho, iota, etc. -- recognized but
  not yet functional)
- Test: tokenize "3 + 4" and dump tokens

### Step 1.2: Scalar parser
- Right-to-left recursive descent parser
- Handle: number literals, binary ops (+, -, *), parentheses
- Monadic minus (negate)
- Build AST nodes from pre-allocated pool
- Test: parse "2 + 3 * 4" -> correct R-to-L tree

### Step 1.3: Scalar evaluator
- Tree-walking evaluator for scalar expressions
- Arithmetic: add, subtract, multiply
- Division: software divide (COR24 has no div instruction)
- Modulo via software divide
- Test: "2 + 3 * 4" -> 14 (right-to-left: 2 + 12)

### Step 1.4: REPL loop
- Read line -> tokenize -> parse -> eval -> print result
- Error recovery: on any error, print message, discard line, re-prompt
- Test: interactive scalar calculator works

### Step 1.5: Variables
- Symbol table (64 entries, linear scan)
- Assignment: `A <- expr`
- Variable reference in expressions
- Test: `A <- 5` then `A + 3` -> 8

---

## Phase 2: Vector Support

### Step 2.1: Array data structure
- Heap allocation (bump allocator)
- Array header: rank, shape, size, data pointer
- `new_array(rank, shape)` allocator
- `array_get(arr, i)` and `array_set(arr, i, val)` accessors

### Step 2.2: Vector literals
- Parser recognizes adjacent numbers as vector: `1 2 3`
- Tokenizer produces sequence of NUM tokens
- Parser groups consecutive numbers into a vector node
- Test: `1 2 3` creates a 3-element vector

### Step 2.3: Element-wise operations
- Extend evaluator for vector + vector
- Scalar extension: scalar + vector, vector + scalar
- Conformability check (LENGTH ERROR on mismatch)
- All arithmetic ops: +, -, *, comparisons
- Test: `1 2 3 + 10 20 30` -> `11 22 33`

### Step 2.4: Vector output
- Print vectors: space-separated values on one line
- Right-justify numbers to consistent width
- Test: display various vectors correctly

---

## Phase 3: Core APL Primitives

### Step 3.1: iota
- Monadic: `iota N` generates vector 0 1 2 ... N-1
- Allocate N-element vector, fill with indices
- Test: `iota 5` -> `0 1 2 3 4`

### Step 3.2: rho (reshape and shape-of)
- Monadic: `rho A` returns shape as a vector
- Dyadic: `S rho A` reshapes A to shape S (with cyclic fill)
- Test: `2 3 rho iota 6` -> 2x3 matrix display
- Test: `rho 1 2 3` -> `3`

### Step 3.3: Reduce
- `+/`, `-/`, `*/` over vectors
- Right-to-left reduction (standard APL)
- Test: `+/ iota 10` -> `45`
- Test: `-/ 1 2 3` -> `1 - (2 - 3)` = `1 - (-1)` = `2`

### Step 3.4: take and drop
- Dyadic: `N take A` returns first N elements
- Dyadic: `N drop A` returns all but first N elements
- Negative N: take/drop from end
- Test: `3 take iota 10` -> `0 1 2`
- Test: `_2 take iota 5` -> `3 4`

### Step 3.5: rev (reverse) and cat (catenate/ravel)
- Monadic `rev`: reverse a vector
- Monadic `cat`: ravel (flatten to 1D)
- Dyadic `cat`: catenate two vectors
- Test: `rev iota 5` -> `4 3 2 1 0`
- Test: `(iota 3) cat (iota 3)` -> `0 1 2 0 1 2`

---

## Phase 4: Matrix Support

### Step 4.1: Matrix creation
- `rho` with 2-element shape creates matrix
- Internal storage: row-major flat array
- Test: `2 3 rho 1 2 3 4 5 6` creates 2x3 matrix

### Step 4.2: Matrix display
- One row per line
- Right-justified columns
- Pre-scan for column widths
- Test: matrices display with aligned columns

### Step 4.3: Matrix operations
- Element-wise ops on conformable matrices
- Scalar extension to matrix
- `rho` of matrix returns 2-element vector
- `cat` (ravel) on matrix returns vector
- Test: matrix + matrix, scalar + matrix

### Step 4.4: take/drop on matrices
- `N take M` takes first N rows
- `N drop M` drops first N rows
- Test: `1 take 2 3 rho iota 6` -> first row as matrix

---

## Phase 5: Polish and Testing

### Step 5.1: System commands
- `)CLEAR` -- reset heap and symbol table
- `)VARS` -- list defined variable names
- `)OFF` -- halt interpreter
- Test: each command works correctly

### Step 5.2: Error handling
- SYNTAX ERROR for malformed input
- LENGTH ERROR for non-conformable shapes
- RANK ERROR for unsupported rank combinations
- DOMAIN ERROR for division by zero
- WS FULL for heap exhaustion
- VALUE ERROR for undefined variables
- Test: each error type triggers correctly and REPL recovers

### Step 5.3: Edge cases
- Empty vectors (iota 0)
- Single-element arrays
- Very large arrays (stress test heap)
- Deeply nested parentheses
- Long input lines

### Step 5.4: Conformance test suite
- Comprehensive test script fed via cor24-run
- Expected output comparison
- Document any deviations from standard APL behavior

### Step 5.5: Documentation
- Update README.md with usage instructions
- Document supported operations and syntax
- Example session transcript
- Known limitations

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-setup-repo-structure [production]: Create the project directory structure and build system. Create src/ for C so...
  [x] 002-uart-io-bootstrap [legacy]: Write uart_putchar, uart_getchar, uart_puts, uart_getline with backspace support. Test echo and startup banner. (Phase 0, Step 0.2)
  [x] 003-number-io [legacy]: Write print_int and parse_int for decimal I/O with underscore-negative. Test round-trip. (Phase 0, Step 0.3)
  [x] 004-tokenizer [legacy]: Implement lexer with token types NUM, IDENT, RESERVED, operators, ASSIGN, parens. Reserved word lookup. (Phase 1, Step 1.1)
  [x] 005-scalar-parser [legacy]: Right-to-left recursive descent parser for scalar expressions with +, -, *, parens, monadic negate. (Phase 1, Step 1.2)
  [x] 006-scalar-evaluator [legacy]: Tree-walking evaluator for scalar arithmetic including software divide. (Phase 1, Step 1.3)
  [x] 007-scalar-repl [legacy]: Wire up REPL loop: read line, tokenize, parse, eval, print. Error recovery. (Phase 1, Step 1.4)
  [x] 008-variables [legacy]: Symbol table (64 entries), assignment with <-, variable references in expressions. (Phase 1, Step 1.5)
  [x] 009-array-data-structure [legacy]: Heap bump allocator, Array header struct, new_array/get/set accessors. (Phase 2, Step 2.1)
  [x] 010-vector-literals [legacy]: Parser recognizes adjacent numbers as vectors. Create vector nodes in AST. (Phase 2, Step 2.2)
  [x] 011-element-wise-ops [legacy]: Extend evaluator for vector+vector, scalar extension, conformability checks. (Phase 2, Step 2.3)
  [x] 012-vector-output [legacy]: Print vectors space-separated with consistent width. (Phase 2, Step 2.4)
  [x] 013-iota [legacy]: Implement monadic iota N generating 0..N-1 vector. (Phase 3, Step 3.1)
  [x] 014-rho [legacy]: Implement monadic rho (shape-of) and dyadic rho (reshape with cyclic fill). (Phase 3, Step 3.2)
  [x] 015-reduce [legacy]: Implement +/ -/ */ reduce operators over vectors, right-to-left. (Phase 3, Step 3.3)
  [x] 016-take-drop [legacy]: Implement dyadic take and drop with negative-N support. (Phase 3, Step 3.4)
  [x] 017-rev-cat [legacy]: Implement monadic rev (reverse), monadic cat (ravel), dyadic cat (catenate). (Phase 3, Step 3.5)
  [x] 018-matrix-creation [legacy]: Extend rho for 2-element shapes creating rank-2 matrices. Row-major storage. (Phase 4, Step 4.1)
  [ ] 019-matrix-display [legacy]: Matrix output with one row per line, right-justified aligned columns. (Phase 4, Step 4.2) <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 020-matrix-ops [legacy]: Element-wise ops on matrices, scalar extension to matrices, rho/cat on matrices. (Phase 4, Step 4.3)
  [ ] 021-matrix-take-drop [legacy]: take/drop on matrices operating on rows. (Phase 4, Step 4.4)
  [ ] 022-system-commands [legacy]: Implement )CLEAR, )VARS, )OFF system commands. (Phase 5, Step 5.1)
  [ ] 023-error-handling [legacy]: SYNTAX, LENGTH, RANK, DOMAIN, WS FULL, VALUE errors with recovery. (Phase 5, Step 5.2)
  [ ] 024-edge-cases [legacy]: Empty vectors, single-element arrays, large arrays, nested parens, long lines. (Phase 5, Step 5.3)
  [ ] 025-test-suite [legacy]: Comprehensive conformance test script with expected output comparison. (Phase 5, Step 5.4)
  [ ] 026-final-docs [legacy]: Update README, document syntax and operations, example session, known limitations. (Phase 5, Step 5.5)
  [ ] 027-027-quad-led [production]: Implement qled quad system variable for LED D2 hardware I/O. Add TOK_QLED to ...
  [ ] 028-028-quad-sw [production]: Implement qsw quad system variable for switch S2 hardware I/O. Add TOK_QSW to...
  [ ] 029-029-quad-exprs [production]: Verify quad variables work in arbitrary expression contexts. Test qled <- qsw...
  [ ] 030-030-qsvo-system-fn [production]: Implement □SVO system function. Tokenizer recognizes qSVO as TOK_QSVO. Dyad...
  [ ] 031-031-shared-var-indexing [production]: Implement indexed read/write on shared variables. MMIO[N] reads byte at FF000...
  [ ] 032-032-graceful-degradation [production]: Implement graceful degradation pattern. Verify →(MMIORC<2)/NODEV branch-aro...
  [ ] 033-033-bracket-indexing [production]: Implement bracket indexing on vectors. V[N] reads element N. V[N]<-expr write...
  [ ] 034-034-bitwise-ops [production]: Implement bitwise operations: and (AND), or (OR), not (NOT). Reserved words l...
  [ ] 035-035-branch-labels [production]: Implement branch (→) and labels. →N branches to line N, →LABEL to label...
  [ ] 036-036-multiline-programs [production]: Implement multi-line program storage. Line array in heap. [N] expr syntax to ...
  [ ] 037-037-user-functions [production]: Implement user-defined functions. del R<-FN X ... del (ASCII for ∇). Monadi...
  [ ] 038-038-apl-image-format [production]: Define and implement APL image format. Newline-separated lines, null-terminat...
  [ ] 039-039-batch-mode [production]: Implement batch mode detection and reader. Check known address for non-zero i...
  [ ] 040-040-uart-echo-demo [production]: Build and test the full UART echo demo. APL batch program: echoes UART in to ...
  [ ] 041-041-comparison-ops [production]: Comparison operators (= >= <= > < !=) returning 0/1 boolean vectors. Element-wise on vectors. Tokenize as new operator tokens, evaluate to integer 0 or 1. Test: 3 = 3 -> 1, 1 2 3 >= 2 -> 0 1 1. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 042-042-max-min [production]: Dyadic ceil (max) and floor (min) primitives. Add as reserved words. Extend reduce to support ceil/ and floor/ for max-reduce and min-reduce. Test: 3 ceil 5 -> 5, ceil/ 4 1 7 2 -> 7. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 043-043-compress [production]: Boolean compress operator. MASK compress VECTOR selects elements where mask is 1. New reserved word or syntax. Test: 1 0 1 compress 10 20 30 -> 10 30. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 044-044-char-type [production]: Character data type and string literals. Extend tokenizer for single-quoted strings. Store as char vectors in heap with type flag. Print char vectors as strings. Test: 'hello' prints hello. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 045-045-string-ops [production]: String operations: rho on characters (5 rho '#' -> '#####'), cat joining strings ('hi' cat ' world'). Extend existing rho and cat for character arrays. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 046-046-nested-arrays [production]: Boxed/nested arrays. Vector-of-strings creation via stranding: 'A' 'B' 'C'. Boxed storage where each element is a heap pointer. Nested vector display. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 047-047-pick [production]: Pick operator (pick) for nested vector indexing. I pick V returns the I-th boxed element. Test: 2 pick 'cat' 'dog' 'fish' -> 'fish'. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 048-048-prng-roll [production]: PRNG with LCG algorithm. Roll operator: roll N returns random 1..N, element-wise on vectors. qRL quad variable for seed. Auto-seed from UART keystroke timing. See docs/randomness.md. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 049-049-fmt-format [production]: Format operator (fmt). fmt N converts integer to character vector. fmt V converts vector to string. Used for: 'ROUND ' cat fmt N. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 050-050-quad-output [production]: Quad output (qout). qout <- expr prints value with newline. Needed for explicit output inside functions. Currently output is implicit from REPL eval. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 051-051-delay [production]: Delay function qDL. qDL N pauses for N milliseconds. Implement as calibrated spin loop or timer register read. Integer ms (no float). (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 052-052-comments [production]: Line comments. # at start of token position skips to end of line. Allows documenting APL programs. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 053-053-or-reduce [production]: Extend reduce for or/ (logical OR reduce) and and/ (logical AND reduce). Test: or/ 0 0 1 0 -> 1, and/ 1 1 0 -> 0. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 054-054-horse-race-test [production]: Transliterate and run both horse race APL programs on COR24 emulator. Verify output matches GNU APL behavior. Milestone: full APL programs running on custom interpreter. (Horse race milestone)

=== CURRENT STEP: 019-matrix-display [legacy] ===
Status: pending
Description: Matrix output with one row per line, right-justified aligned columns. (Phase 4, Step 4.2)

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

=== PLAN ===
# COR24 APL Interpreter -- Implementation Plan

## Phase Overview

| Phase | Description                    | Deliverable                       |
|-------|-------------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| 0     | Project setup and toolchain   | Build system, hello-world on emu  |
| 1     | Scalar REPL                   | Read, eval, print scalar exprs    |
| 2     | Vector support                | 1D arrays, element-wise ops       |
| 3     | Core APL primitives           | iota, rho, reduce, take, drop     |
| 4     | Matrix support                | 2D arrays, formatted output       |
| 5     | Polish and testing            | Error handling, edge cases, docs  |

---

## Phase 0: Project Setup and Toolchain Validation

### Step 0.1: Repository structure
- Create `src/` directory for C source files
- Create `asm/` directory for hand-written assembly helpers
- Create `test/` directory for test scripts
- Create `Makefile` or build script for tc24r + cor24-run pipeline
- Verify tc24r can compile a trivial C program to COR24 assembly
- Verify cor24-run can assemble and execute the result

### Step 0.2: UART I/O bootstrap
- Write `uart_putchar()` and `uart_getchar()` in C (or asm)
- Write `uart_puts()` for string output
- Write `uart_getline()` with backspace support
- Test: print "COR24 APL v0.1" on startup, echo typed lines

### Step 0.3: Number I/O
- Write `print_int()` -- integer to decimal string via UART
- Write `parse_int()` -- decimal string to integer
- Handle negative numbers (underscore prefix `_N`)
- Test: type a number, see it echoed back

---

## Phase 1: Scalar REPL

### Step 1.1: Tokenizer
- Implement token types: NUM, PLUS, MINUS, STAR, SLASH, LPAREN,
  RPAREN, ASSIGN, IDENT, RESERVED, EOL
- Scan input buffer into token array
- Reserved word lookup table (rho, iota, etc. -- recognized but
  not yet functional)
- Test: tokenize "3 + 4" and dump tokens

### Step 1.2: Scalar parser
- Right-to-left recursive descent parser
- Handle: number literals, binary ops (+, -, *), parentheses
- Monadic minus (negate)
- Build AST nodes from pre-allocated pool
- Test: parse "2 + 3 * 4" -> correct R-to-L tree

### Step 1.3: Scalar evaluator
- Tree-walking evaluator for scalar expressions
- Arithmetic: add, subtract, multiply
- Division: software divide (COR24 has no div instruction)
- Modulo via software divide
- Test: "2 + 3 * 4" -> 14 (right-to-left: 2 + 12)

### Step 1.4: REPL loop
- Read line -> tokenize -> parse -> eval -> print result
- Error recovery: on any error, print message, discard line, re-prompt
- Test: interactive scalar calculator works

### Step 1.5: Variables
- Symbol table (64 entries, linear scan)
- Assignment: `A <- expr`
- Variable reference in expressions
- Test: `A <- 5` then `A + 3` -> 8

---

## Phase 2: Vector Support

### Step 2.1: Array data structure
- Heap allocation (bump allocator)
- Array header: rank, shape, size, data pointer
- `new_array(rank, shape)` allocator
- `array_get(arr, i)` and `array_set(arr, i, val)` accessors

### Step 2.2: Vector literals
- Parser recognizes adjacent numbers as vector: `1 2 3`
- Tokenizer produces sequence of NUM tokens
- Parser groups consecutive numbers into a vector node
- Test: `1 2 3` creates a 3-element vector

### Step 2.3: Element-wise operations
- Extend evaluator for vector + vector
- Scalar extension: scalar + vector, vector + scalar
- Conformability check (LENGTH ERROR on mismatch)
- All arithmetic ops: +, -, *, comparisons
- Test: `1 2 3 + 10 20 30` -> `11 22 33`

### Step 2.4: Vector output
- Print vectors: space-separated values on one line
- Right-justify numbers to consistent width
- Test: display various vectors correctly

---

## Phase 3: Core APL Primitives

### Step 3.1: iota
- Monadic: `iota N` generates vector 0 1 2 ... N-1
- Allocate N-element vector, fill with indices
- Test: `iota 5` -> `0 1 2 3 4`

### Step 3.2: rho (reshape and shape-of)
- Monadic: `rho A` returns shape as a vector
- Dyadic: `S rho A` reshapes A to shape S (with cyclic fill)
- Test: `2 3 rho iota 6` -> 2x3 matrix display
- Test: `rho 1 2 3` -> `3`

### Step 3.3: Reduce
- `+/`, `-/`, `*/` over vectors
- Right-to-left reduction (standard APL)
- Test: `+/ iota 10` -> `45`
- Test: `-/ 1 2 3` -> `1 - (2 - 3)` = `1 - (-1)` = `2`

### Step 3.4: take and drop
- Dyadic: `N take A` returns first N elements
- Dyadic: `N drop A` returns all but first N elements
- Negative N: take/drop from end
- Test: `3 take iota 10` -> `0 1 2`
- Test: `_2 take iota 5` -> `3 4`

### Step 3.5: rev (reverse) and cat (catenate/ravel)
- Monadic `rev`: reverse a vector
- Monadic `cat`: ravel (flatten to 1D)
- Dyadic `cat`: catenate two vectors
- Test: `rev iota 5` -> `4 3 2 1 0`
- Test: `(iota 3) cat (iota 3)` -> `0 1 2 0 1 2`

---

## Phase 4: Matrix Support

### Step 4.1: Matrix creation
- `rho` with 2-element shape creates matrix
- Internal storage: row-major flat array
- Test: `2 3 rho 1 2 3 4 5 6` creates 2x3 matrix

### Step 4.2: Matrix display
- One row per line
- Right-justified columns
- Pre-scan for column widths
- Test: matrices display with aligned columns

### Step 4.3: Matrix operations
- Element-wise ops on conformable matrices
- Scalar extension to matrix
- `rho` of matrix returns 2-element vector
- `cat` (ravel) on matrix returns vector
- Test: matrix + matrix, scalar + matrix

### Step 4.4: take/drop on matrices
- `N take M` takes first N rows
- `N drop M` drops first N rows
- Test: `1 take 2 3 rho iota 6` -> first row as matrix

---

## Phase 5: Polish and Testing

### Step 5.1: System commands
- `)CLEAR` -- reset heap and symbol table
- `)VARS` -- list defined variable names
- `)OFF` -- halt interpreter
- Test: each command works correctly

### Step 5.2: Error handling
- SYNTAX ERROR for malformed input
- LENGTH ERROR for non-conformable shapes
- RANK ERROR for unsupported rank combinations
- DOMAIN ERROR for division by zero
- WS FULL for heap exhaustion
- VALUE ERROR for undefined variables
- Test: each error type triggers correctly and REPL recovers

### Step 5.3: Edge cases
- Empty vectors (iota 0)
- Single-element arrays
- Very large arrays (stress test heap)
- Deeply nested parentheses
- Long input lines

### Step 5.4: Conformance test suite
- Comprehensive test script fed via cor24-run
- Expected output comparison
- Document any deviations from standard APL behavior

### Step 5.5: Documentation
- Update README.md with usage instructions
- Document supported operations and syntax
- Example session transcript
- Known limitations

  [ ] 032-032-graceful-degradation [production]: Implement graceful degradation pattern. Verify →(MMIORC<2)/NODEV branch-aro...
  [ ] 033-033-bracket-indexing [production]: Implement bracket indexing on vectors. V[N] reads element N. V[N]<-expr write...
  [ ] 034-034-bitwise-ops [production]: Implement bitwise operations: and (AND), or (OR), not (NOT). Reserved words l...
  [ ] 035-035-branch-labels [production]: Implement branch (→) and labels. →N branches to line N, →LABEL to label...
  [ ] 036-036-multiline-programs [production]: Implement multi-line program storage. Line array in heap. [N] expr syntax to ...
  [ ] 037-037-user-functions [production]: Implement user-defined functions. del R<-FN X ... del (ASCII for ∇). Monadi...
  [ ] 038-038-apl-image-format [production]: Define and implement APL image format. Newline-separated lines, null-terminat...
  [ ] 039-039-batch-mode [production]: Implement batch mode detection and reader. Check known address for non-zero i...
  [ ] 040-040-uart-echo-demo [production]: Build and test the full UART echo demo. APL batch program: echoes UART in to ...
  [ ] 041-041-comparison-ops [production]: Comparison operators (= >= <= > < !=) returning 0/1 boolean vectors. Element-wise on vectors. Tokenize as new operator tokens, evaluate to integer 0 or 1. Test: 3 = 3 -> 1, 1 2 3 >= 2 -> 0 1 1. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 042-042-max-min [production]: Dyadic ceil (max) and floor (min) primitives. Add as reserved words. Extend reduce to support ceil/ and floor/ for max-reduce and min-reduce. Test: 3 ceil 5 -> 5, ceil/ 4 1 7 2 -> 7. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 043-043-compress [production]: Boolean compress operator. MASK compress VECTOR selects elements where mask is 1. New reserved word or syntax. Test: 1 0 1 compress 10 20 30 -> 10 30. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 044-044-char-type [production]: Character data type and string literals. Extend tokenizer for single-quoted strings. Store as char vectors in heap with type flag. Print char vectors as strings. Test: 'hello' prints hello. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 045-045-string-ops [production]: String operations: rho on characters (5 rho '#' -> '#####'), cat joining strings ('hi' cat ' world'). Extend existing rho and cat for character arrays. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 046-046-nested-arrays [production]: Boxed/nested arrays. Vector-of-strings creation via stranding: 'A' 'B' 'C'. Boxed storage where each element is a heap pointer. Nested vector display. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 047-047-pick [production]: Pick operator (pick) for nested vector indexing. I pick V returns the I-th boxed element. Test: 2 pick 'cat' 'dog' 'fish' -> 'fish'. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 048-048-prng-roll [production]: PRNG with LCG algorithm. Roll operator: roll N returns random 1..N, element-wise on vectors. qRL quad variable for seed. Auto-seed from UART keystroke timing. See docs/randomness.md. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 049-049-fmt-format [production]: Format operator (fmt). fmt N converts integer to character vector. fmt V converts vector to string. Used for: 'ROUND ' cat fmt N. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 050-050-quad-output [production]: Quad output (qout). qout <- expr prints value with newline. Needed for explicit output inside functions. Currently output is implicit from REPL eval. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 051-051-delay [production]: Delay function qDL. qDL N pauses for N milliseconds. Implement as calibrated spin loop or timer register read. Integer ms (no float). (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 052-052-comments [production]: Line comments. # at start of token position skips to end of line. Allows documenting APL programs. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 053-053-or-reduce [production]: Extend reduce for or/ (logical OR reduce) and and/ (logical AND reduce). Test: or/ 0 0 1 0 -> 1, and/ 1 1 0 -> 0. (Horse race prerequisite)
  [ ] 054-054-horse-race-test [production]: Transliterate and run both horse race APL programs on COR24 emulator. Verify output matches GNU APL behavior. Milestone: full APL programs running on custom interpreter. (Horse race milestone)

content.text: Based on the session context, matrix display was already implemented during step 018. Let me verify the current state and then mark this step complete.

Matrix display is already fully implemented in `src/main.c`. The code handles rank-2 arrays with row-per-line output, right-justified columns, and automatic width calculation. This was done as part of the previous step (018-matrix-creation).

Let me verify it works on the emulator.

The implementation is complete and was already validated via UART testing in the previous session (observations #26701, #26702). The display code:
- Extracts rows/cols from rank-2 arrays
- Pre-scans all elements for maximum numeric width
- Prints each row on its own line with right-justified, space-separated values
